title: botrychium tolucaense
intro: Botrychium tolucaense, also known as Toluca rattlesnake fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ophioglossaceae, characterized by its unique, fern-like appearance and distinctive leaf structure. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting wound healing, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. Herbal preparations of Botrychium tolucaense are often made by infusing the dried leaves in hot water to create a tea or by using the plant's rhizome in tincture or capsule form.
